commit.message:
fix(e2e): add CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL to debt-service in docker-compose

The debt-service was missing CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL in its Docker
environment, causing it to use the default http://localhost:8092 —
which inside the Docker network points to the container itself, not
the creditor-service container.

This caused validateCreditorAction() to fail with a connection error
on every claim submission, triggering the Resilience4j circuit breaker
fallback that threw OpenDebtException(CRITICAL), which GlobalExceptionHandler
mapped to HTTP 503. The creditor-portal then showed AFVIST with
'503: Debt service unavailable', failing FR-P012-02b.

Fix: add CREDITOR_SERVICE_URL: http://creditor-service:8092 alongside
the already-correct CASE_SERVICE_URL pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(eval): Phase 0 — search quality evaluation infrastructure (#9)

* feat: implement Phase 3 e-commerce heuristics and related product pruning

* docs: modernize OUTPUT_CONTRACT and EXTRACTION wiki references

* feat(core): modernize extraction engine, eradicate panics, and add cloud jina opt-in

This commit resolves major architectural goals by hardening execution stability,
improving text-extraction heuristics, and enforcing privacy-by-default routing:

1. Panic Eradication & Error Handling
   - System-wide removal of `unwrap()` and `expect()`, replacing them with safe

     calls to graceful `anyhow::bail!` error bubbling.

2. AST-Based Semantic Pruning
   - Replaced fragile character-counting link saturation heuristics with mathematical
     AST calculations via the `tl` crate (`evaluate_block_saturation`).
   - Accurately computes link density by evaluating structural DOM nodes,
     significantly improving the accuracy of sidebar and navigation pruning.

3. Privacy by Default
   - Added an explicit `--allow-cloud` CLI flag to gate third-party
     extraction proxies (Jina.ai).
   - Forces `ripweb` to strictly adhere to localized parsing and heuristic fallbacks
     unless explicitly given permission to use offsite JS rendering engines.

commit.message:
fix: align DepthRenderer readiness check with rendering logic (#18266)

## Problem

`DepthRenderer.customIsReadyFunction` checks `isReady()` for all
submeshes, but `DepthRenderer.isReady()` returns `false` for materials
with `disableDepthWrite`. The actual `renderSubMesh` function skips
those submeshes entirely. This mismatch causes `scene.whenReadyAsync()`
to never resolve when any mesh in the scene has `disableDepthWrite =
true`.

Forum report: https://forum.babylonjs.com/t/63104

## Fix

Added the same skip conditions from `renderSubMesh` (no material,
`infiniteDistance`, `disableDepthWrite`, zero vertices) as `continue`
guards in `customIsReadyFunction`.

## Tests

Added unit tests in
`packages/dev/core/test/unit/Rendering/babylon.depthRenderer.test.ts`.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix json_quote dropping ByteConcat tail when quoting (#7)

## Problem

`json_quote` silently truncates regexes built from string literals
longer than 31 bytes (`MAX_BYTE_CONCAT`) when those literals contain
characters that need JSON escaping (`\n`, `\r`, `\t`, `\\\\`, `"`,
control chars, `\x7F`).

Long literals are internally split into chained `ByteConcat(bytes,
tail)` nodes. When `json_quote` encounters a chunk whose bytes include a
character needing quoting, it splits and re-encodes those bytes — but
drops `args[0]` (the mapped tail), discarding everything after that
chunk.

The `else` branches correctly preserve the tail; only the quoting branch
was missing it.

Reported in guidance-ai/llguidance#326.

## Fix

Append `args[0]` (the mapped tail expression) to `acc` before calling
`_mk_concat_vec`, matching the behavior of the other branches.

## Test

Added `test_json_quote_long_literal_with_newline` — a 78-byte literal
with `\n` at byte offset 43. Before the fix, the json-quoted regex fails
to match the correctly escaped output. After the fix, it matches.

All existing tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: skip JIT execution in TailCall handler (compile-only)

TailCall is a terminal opcode that Maglev/Turbofan emit Deoptimize for.
Attempting try_execute_best_jit on every tail-call iteration corrupts
thread-local state via deopt side-effects and produces wrong results
for recursive tail-call functions.

Keep compilation triggers so JIT code is ready for function entry
(run_dispatch), but skip execution during tail calls.

commit.message:
feat(search): session cohesion boost, improved importance/vector/graph scoring

Session cohesion: thoughts within ±5 positions of a high-scoring
lexical seed (lexical >= 3.0) receive a proximity boost up to 0.5,
decaying linearly with distance. This surfaces evidence turns adjacent
to the matching turn (key for LoCoMo-style multi-turn QA).

Importance scoring: replace flat 3.0x multiplier with a differential
boost proportional to lexical score. Thoughts with lexical signal get
lexical * (importance - 0.5) * 0.3; thoughts with no lexical match
get a small flat (importance - 0.5) * 0.1 bonus.

Vector-lexical fusion: replace step-function boosts with a smooth
exponential decay (VECTOR_ONLY_BOOST=40, VECTOR_DECAY_RATE=2.0) so
vector contribution tapers naturally as lexical strengthens.

Graph scoring:
- ContinuesFrom relation kind boost raised 0.12 → 0.30 (primary
  chain-traversal edge for sequential session data)
- Corrects/Invalidates raised 0.18 → 0.25
- Supersedes raised 0.16 → 0.22, DerivedFrom 0.14 → 0.20
- Graph proximity score raised 0.3/depth → 1.0/depth
- Seed support per extra seed raised 0.05 → 0.1, max seeds 3 → 5
- MAX_GRAPH_SEEDS cap of 20 seeds to bound BFS startup cost

Add session_cohesion field to RankedSearchScore for observability.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(search): skip high-document-frequency terms in BM25 scoring

Terms that appear in >50% of documents (when corpus >= 20 docs) are
near-stopwords and contribute noise rather than signal to BM25 ranking.
Skip them early in the scoring loop to keep retrieval precise.

Fix: Data race in os_process_get() and os_process_find_by_name()

Fixes critical issue where functions returned pointers to internal process table
entries after releasing the mutex, causing potential use-after-free conditions
when the table was modified by concurrent tasks.

Solution: Changed API to copy process data into caller-provided buffers instead
of returning internal pointers. Callers must now pass a process_t* buffer.

Sprint 2 

* Sprint 2: T2.1 - Mejorar Precisión del Análisis de Patrones (EN PROGRESO)

* T2.1: Agregar 16+ patrones de velas de IG.com - Cobertura completa de patrones

* Sprint 2: Mejoras de Calidad y ML - T2.1 a T2.4 completadas
T2.1 - Asegurar Calidad de Datos para ML

  • DataQualityMetrics: Validación OHLCV, outliers, gaps, scoring 0-100

T2.2 - Análisis de Sentimiento Multiidioma
  • newsSentimentService.ts: Análisis EN/ES con traducción automática
  • 50+ palabras clave ponderadas en cada idioma
  • Intensificadores y negadores para inversión de sentimiento
  • Scores normalizados -1 a 1 con confianza 0-100
T2.3 - Predicción de Movimientos Intraday
  • intradayPredictionService.ts: Heurísticas + pattern matching
  • Análisis: Momentum (RSI), Volatilidad (ATR), Tendencia (SMA)
  • Support/Resistance dinámicos, predicciones 4h/8h/24h
  • useIntradayPrediction.ts hook + IntradayPredictionDisplay.tsx
T2.4 - Alertas Dinámicas en Tiempo Real
  • alertService.ts: 5 tipos (Price, %, RSI, MACD, SMA Cross)
  • Notificaciones web + sonido sintetizado
  • localStorage persistencia con validación
  • Rate limiting: 5s cooldown en sonidos, 100 alertas máximo
  • Sanitización de inputs, validación completa
Seguridad:
  ✓ 8 vulnerabilidades auditadas y solucionadas
  ✓ JSON.parse validación con isValidAlert()
  ✓ Sanitización de symbols/names
  ✓ Input limits: 100 alertas, 10K chars, period [2,200]
  ✓ Logging seguro sin exposición de stacks
  ✓ localStorage quota handling
Código:
  • 7 archivos nuevos (2,130+ líneas)
  • 0 errores TypeScript
  • 100% compatible con AGENTS.md

* fix: Correcciones de Type Safety y Duplicación de Eventos (Sprint 2)
CORRECCIONES REALIZADAS:
1. ✅ alertService.ts - Eliminada duplicación de eventos
   - Cambiar checkAllAlerts() a función 'pura' sin notificar observadores
   - Agregar método processTriggerEvents() para notificaciones centralizadas
   - Remover suscripción redundante en useAlerts hook
2. ✅ useIntradayPrediction.ts - Corregidas condiciones de validación
   - Cambiar validación !currentPrice a: currentPrice === undefined || Number.isNaN(currentPrice)
   - Permite que price 0 sea válido
   - Resetear loading/error en early return para evitar UI stuck
3. ✅ IntradayPredictionDisplay.tsx - Null check explícito
   - Cambiar prediction.priceTarget && a: prediction.priceTarget != null
   - Permite renderizar price target de 0 como valor válido
4. ✅ intradayPredictionService.ts - Timeframe parameter now used
   - Mapear input timeframe a output predicción (4h/8h/24h)
   - Short timeframes (1m-1h) → 4h prediction
   - Medium (4h) → 8h prediction
   - Long (1d+) → 24h prediction
   - Actualizar useIntradayPrediction hook para pasar timeframe
5. ✅ intradayPredictionService.ts - Type safety en findSimilarHistoricalPatterns
   - Ampliar return type: 'up' | 'down' → 'up' | 'down' | 'neutral'
   - Remover unsafe 'as any' cast
   - Manejar 'neutral' explícitamente en generatePrediction
6. ✅ intradayPredictionService.ts - Guard defensivo en calculateSupportResistance
   - Agregar validación: currentCandles.length === 0
   - Fallback a historicalCandles si currentCandles está vacío
   - Throw error claro si ambos están vacíos
7. ✅ intradayPredictionService.ts - Corregida escalado de probabilidad
   - Cambiar fórmula: Math.abs(probabilityUp - 50) * 2 (max 60)
   - A: ((probabilityUp - 20) / 60) * 100 (max 100)
   - Ahora documentación coincide con implementación (0-100)
8. ✅ newsSentimentService.ts - Remover código muerto de idioma 'other'
   - Cambiar detectLanguage return type: 'en' | 'es' | 'other' → 'en' | 'es'
   - Remover branch 'other' en analyzeSentiment() (código muerto)
   - Remover método translateSpanishToEnglish() (no usada)
   - Simplificar generateReasoning() logic
9. ✅ newsSentimentService.ts - Normalización de diacríticos españoles
   - Agregar normalizeDiacritics() method (NFD + removing combining marks)
   - Cambiar all .replace(/[^\w]/g, '') a normalizeDiacritics() calls
   - Ahora 'crítico' matchea correctamente con 'critico' en diccionarios
10. ✅ candleAnalysisService.ts - Corrección de conteos en documentación
    - 1-vela: 5 → 6 total
    - 2-velas: 9 → 8 total
    - 3-velas: 7 → 6 total
IMPACTO:
- Type safety mejorada (sin 'as any' casts)
- Eliminada duplicación de eventos (eventos exactamente una sola vez)
- Manejo correcto de edge cases (price 0, arrays vacíos, diacríticos)
- API contracts coherentes con implementación
- Código muerto eliminado
TESTING:
- No hay regresiones en compilación
- Todos los warning previos se mantienen (no relacionados a estos cambios)
- Comportamiento funcional preservado

* fix: Address all PR review feedback (Sprint 2)

- intradayPredictionService: Fix calculateMomentum/calculateVolatility
  off-by-one (divided by 14 but computed 13 samples; now divide by
  changes.length / trCount)
- intradayPredictionService: Treat movePercent==0 as 'neutral' (not 'down')
  in findSimilarHistoricalPatterns
- intradayPredictionService: Replace string/any params in generatePrediction
  with proper union types; remove 'as any' casts in factors return

commit.message:
feat: make AdLib/OPL formats editable with OPL3Synth

Phase 1 of editable AdLib formats:

- Convert AdPlugParser from FurnaceOPL to OPL3 instruments
  - New applyOPLRegisters() helper maps 11-byte OPL register data to OPL3Config
  - All 4 format-specific instrument blocks (RAD, HSC, DRO/IMF, CMF) converted
  - Instruments now use the existing OPL3Synth (noteOn/noteOff, editable params)

- Fix routing so TS-parsed formats reach AdPlugParser
  - writeHandlers: detect format.nativeParser and call parseAdPlugFile directly
  - parseModuleToSong: try AdPlug parser before AmigaFormatParsers/UADE fallback
  - Previously RAD/HSC/DRO/IMF/CMF fell through to UADE (which can't play them)

- Formats now editable: RAD v1/v2, HSC, DRO v1/v2, IMF, CMF
  - 9 OPL2 channels with pattern data (notes, instruments, effects)
  - OPL3Synth instruments with full per-operator ADSR/level/waveform editing
  - Pattern positions, speed, BPM preserved from original files
